mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day return frequency during top PAYEMS growth quartiles is less than 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 0.5 percent, the zero lower bound environment forces investors into risk assets and compresses discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with falling unemployment jointly signal a robust demand-labor virtuous cycle that boosts corporate earnings visibility, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint z-score combination of RSAFS and UNRATE fails to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of observations over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation regime, accelerating consumer prices paired with rising industrial production amplify margin compression fears, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month as equity risk premiums expand.
failure_condition: During high-inflation trigger months, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time, or the interaction term shows no negative alpha (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month contraction in industrial production signals deteriorating corporate output and pricing power, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Negative month-over-month INDPRO changes do not coincide with negative S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1,200 thousand units, the sharp slowdown in residential investment foreshadows broader demand weakness, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following HOUST below 1200 are not significantly negative compared to baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness triggers wage pressure concerns and fears of monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns following UNRATE below 5.0% are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) versus neutral periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP confirms the economy is operating above potential, supporting positive earnings revisions and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth surprises do not coincide with positive S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 55% of observations over a rolling 3-year window.
